_meta.title: Build A Regex Engine Thompson Construction

questions.question: In Thompson's construction, how many outgoing ε-transitions can any single state have?
questions.explanation: Thompson's construction guarantees at most two outgoing ε-transitions from any state. The union case creates a new start state with ε-transitions to two sub-NFAs, which is the maximum.

questions.question: What is the worst-case time complexity of simulating a Thompson NFA with |Q| states on a text of length n?
questions.explanation: At each of n input positions, we maintain a set of at most |Q| states. For each state, computing the next set takes O(|Q|) transitions. The ε-closure step is also O(|Q|). Total: O(n × |Q|).

questions.question: Why can catastrophic backtracking occur in recursive-descent (backtracking) regex engines like PCRE but NOT in Thompson NFA simulation?
options: Thompson NFA uses caching while backtracking does not
Thompson NFA explores all paths in parallel via state sets, bounded by the number of NFA states
Backtracking engines are slower because they use more memory
Thompson NFA converts to a DFA before matching
questions.explanation: Thompson NFA simulation keeps track of all possible current states as a set. The set size is bounded by |Q|, so each step takes O(|Q|) work. Backtracking engines try paths one at a time and may explore exponentially many paths for patterns like (a+)+b.

questions.question: The regex `a*` is desugared into which Thompson NFA structure?
options: A new start/accept pair with ε from start to accept (skip), ε from accept back to start of `a`, and ε from accept of `a` to the new accept
A single state with a self-loop on `a`
questions.explanation: Thompson's Kleene star construction creates new start and accept states. It adds: ε from new start to inner start (enter), ε from new start to new accept (skip zero times), ε from inner accept back to inner start (repeat), and ε from inner accept to new accept (exit).

questions.question: What does Thompson's construction produce from a regular expression?
options: An epsilon-NFA that recognizes the same language as the regex
A parse tree of the regex
questions.explanation: Thompson's construction recursively builds an epsilon-NFA from a regular expression using simple rules for concatenation, alternation (|), and Kleene star (*). Each rule creates small NFA fragments that are joined with epsilon transitions.
